brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with valid computation; goes one level beyond surface by distinguishing observational vs causal interpretation; explicitly monitors reasoning via suppress/amplify signals and notes association vs causation distinction; verifies marginals sum correctly and joint probabilities are consistent; acknowledges this is conditional probability not causal claim but doesn't deeply quantify uncertainty; mentions causal vs associational distinction but doesn't systematically evaluate alternative interpretations; full derivation chain from joint probabilities to conditionals to comparison is clearly shown and reproducible.
